post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 192: Disney Mafia

If you're not currently alive in this game, do not post. The game is over, you can post.

Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas among others for the rule template.
  • Rules
    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications or anything else.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless you are specifically given permission to do so.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • Hide tags are allowed as long as they conform to the above rule.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted, even for things like typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • Do not post in color.
    • Do not abuse loopholes.

  • Voting and Hanging
    • This game is based on deadline hang. This means that the player with the most votes at the deadline will be hanged.
    • If there is a tie at the deadline, the hang will be randomly chosen from the players with the greatest number of votes.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player reaches a majority of votes, then that player will be hanged.
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fDAs", "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly and do not use nicknames. There is one exception to this; see below.
    • For the purpose of voting, l (lowercase L) and I (uppercase i) are identical.
    • Votes in hide or tip tags will not be counted.
    • Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until the mods say the game is officially over. If you want to purposely fake vote, give the mods a heads up in your role PM so it is not accidentally counted.
    • You may vote for a no hang to occur during day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • If there is an even number of players, half of the players voting for no hang is considered a majority, but only for no hang.
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, but if you wish to, unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post, and the game will proceed into twilight.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once the end-of-day scene is posted, the person hanged is officially dead and their role will be revealed. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • Put effort into the game. The moderators will prod you first and then replace you (36 hours after a prod) if you don't play the game.
    • If the moderators get tired of repeatedly prodding you, you'll be replaced.
    • Each day will be 240 hours (10 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion. Note that this will be unlikely and will usually only happen in circumstances of extreme inactivity or replacements too close to end of day.
    • The deadline will occur the very second 240 hours has occurred.
    • The night deadline will be 48 hours (2 days) after night begins.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or their partner(s) (if they have them), the player will lose their chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Any night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end some time after the night deadline when the mods make the post announcing the end of night. You may not post until the moderators say you can, even if the night deadline has passed. If you've read the rules, tell in your role pm what the first bullet point under activity says.
    • If a player is going to be in V/LA, they must post in bold in-thread or PM the mods. The moderators will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.
    • Players with private chats (for this game) can access them at any time unless otherwise specified.

  • Etiquette
    •  Do not use inappropriate language.
    •  Do not make any personal attacks.
    •  Follow all of the rules.
    •  Be mature.
    •  For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to three warnings, SE’s up to two, and IC’s one. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill-level infraction, they will be modkilled or force-replaced at the mods' discretion. Please do not do anything bad enough to deserve a modkill.

  • Moderators
    • chess24 is the moderator of Mafia Game 192 and cj13609517288 is the other moderator.
    • Don't post in color.
    • The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time.
    • The moderators have the last say in interpreting the rules.
  • Read all of the rules
Flavor (credit to cj13609517288)
chess24 and cj1360591728 are having fun in the Tower of Terror, but suddenly, the elevator crashed into the ground! When the 13 wise people in AoPS-192 gather to see what happened, chess24 and cj13609517288 already died. They are figured that this event is made by the mafia, and they suspect moles in this very team. The safety of AoPS-192 now depends on you.

The reason I prefer hanging xzhang over mymathkid is that while they are both bandwagoning, mymathkid's bandwagoning is that of the completely newbie kind. It is blatant and overt, completely unthinking. It is the most base kind of bandwagoning.

Meanwhile, xzhang is more sophisticated. He knows bandwagoning is scummy. And so, he avoids the overt bandwagoning that mymathkid is doing. But, he hides it by copying other people. Ultimately, he contributes nothing, but he goes about it in a way that hides his bandwagoning.

Merely going off of bandwagoning is not effective in finding scum. Too many newbies do it. But this is why I draw the distinction between the newbie bandwagoning and what xzhang is doing. That is why I find xzhang to be scummy.

On day 2, NDMath's post in response to my scumread on xzhang really stands out to me.
NDMath wrote:
I still don't see any evidence that mymathkid has been bandwagoning.

Fdas's post doesn't make enough sense to me as to why he is viewing players the way he is.

NDMath doesn't address my argument, and instead just brushes it off and says he doesn't understand it. Note that he also doesn't ask for elaboration and seems eager to just have the entire thing forgotten. This gives the impression that NDMath is trying to defend xzhang without doing so overtly.

This makes me think that xzhang and ND are w/w, so if xzhang flips scum, I would see NDMath as very suspicious.

Moreover, after reading through I feel that NDMath's progression on me has been really odd.
NDMath wrote:
My vote will be on fdas for the rest of D1, though I'm struggling on speaking what I'm seeing. You guys do whatever.

On day 1 NDMath started going on a really weird push on me where he wasn't able to articulate any real argument why I was scum. Instead, he just started spouting vague statements and trying to encourage town to vote me. At first I thought that NDMath just really wanted to spur dynamic wagoning to gain information. But his later post puts it in a different light.
NDMath wrote:
Or like when I voted/accused each of Madelyn and bronzefur most people said nothing and those who did said stuff like "no way you can make a solid read off that". This compares to with fdas where people showed they were thinking about voting fdas over litee/yaj which I interpret as my thoughts against him being more valid/making more sense.

What I see here is NDMath fishing for someone town finds scummy. He tosses out some accusatory statement and if town wasn't receptive he just walks it back without any negative repercussions to himself. Whether or not he proceeds in the attack depends on whether others approve or not. And when he saw less pushback on me, he felt that he had a greater chance so he got more aggressive. But when that stalled NDMath dropped the whole thing since town was no longer receptive.

Throughout the game, NDMath has just been throwing out unsubstantiated comments that my play has been "weird", but never following up with that. But now that day 3 comes along and the nth argument he throws out finally finds traction, ND starts pushing. This reeks of opportunism where he waits until he spots weakness to finally strike. This looks like NDMath has an agenda. His goal is not focused around finding scum, but rather trying to get a specific person hanged. Repeatedly throwing scumreads until one sticks is not how one tries to find scum.


Now, if I take NDMath to be scum, then I feel that Kazakata is quite towny due to the day 3 interactions. The back and forth does not seem like something a pair of mafia would really be doing. It seems more like Kazakata had legitimate questions. So, I don't see them as w/w so I townread Kazakata.


NDMath and Bronze's interactions seem to not be w/w, and avanders interactions with NDMath don't seem to be w/w either and their argument looks like it was natural and not staged. I see avanders as a towny that NDMath managed to convince, and I can understand why avanders has been scumreading me, since my activity this week has not been stellar because of the stuff I had to deal with. Conclusion is I townread avanders.


Charge hasn't been the most active this game, but the posts today feel pretty towny. I'm not ready to form a solid conclusion on Charge yet.


If I were to build a scumteam, I would predict {xzhang, NDMath, Madelyn}. Also throughout the game she has been parroting NDMath in saying that my play has been weird compared to other games, also without providing any evidence or reasoning.

Also, SmartZX has been doing very little this entire game. I would expect more of him since he has played many games in the past. But then I realize, Smart hasn't actually played a town game before. So it would make sense if Smart was town and he had no idea what to do, because this was basically an unknown experience for him. As scum Smart usually does more than this, but being in an unfamiliar situation may be the cause of his timidity.


Now, xzhang is actually very interesting. He has been making a lot of noise throughout the game, and is quite active. However, as I read through the game, I realize that he actually says very little in his many posts. Whenever he makes a read, there is at most a single line explaining it. In no instance does xzhang actually say anything particularly insightful. Moreover, in every instance his reads are in line with town consensus. Everything he says is completely uncontroversial.

Now, in this game we have seen many many examples of blind bandwagoning, most epitomized by mymathkid. xzhang on the other hand, knows very well that bandwagoning is scummy.
xzhang wrote:
Vote: mymathkid that vote for kaz was the last straw. You have been bandwagoning hard the entire game. This being your second game is not an excuse. This is my third game. Make an actual contribution and I might take back this vote

As a scum, xzhang would not overtly bandwagon since he knows it is scummy. But what we see coming out of him is a more subtle kind of bandwagoning. He doesn't bandwagon with his votes mindlessly. Rather, he does what is at the core of bandwagoning, going with the flow. He does nothing to add new material or challenge town consensus. There is the veneer of contribution when in reality there is nothing. He repackages the reads of those around him and hides it better than blind bandwagoning.

While the mindless bandwagoning exhibited by mymathkid is indeed very bad play, such is common among newbies. Rather, the bandwagoning that is truly scummy is the kind that doesn't appear to be bandwagoning on first glance, but instead is able to slip by unnoticed. This is what xzhang has done.

yaj.jhajhria wrote:
My god, there are so many SRs. I'm going to read through them and see if I can make some sense out of it. For now though, it seems to me like the scummiest are Bronzefur, SmartZX, and fdas (in that order).

Here, yaj is basically reiterating what everyone around him has been saying.

Also, SmartZX has been doing very little this entire game. I would expect more of him since he has played many games in the past. But then I realize, Smart hasn't actually played a town game before. So it would make sense if Smart was town and he had no idea what to do, because this was basically an unknown experience for him. As scum Smart usually does more than this, but being in an unfamiliar situation may be the cause of his timidity.


Now, xzhang is actually very interesting. He has been making a lot of noise throughout the game, and is quite active. However, as I read through the game, I realize that he actually says very little in his many posts. Whenever he makes a read, there is at most a single line explaining it. In no instance does xzhang actually say anything particularly insightful. Moreover, in every instance his reads are in line with town consensus. Everything he says is completely uncontroversial.

Now, in this game we have seen many many examples of blind bandwagoning, most epitomized by mymathkid. xzhang on the other hand, knows very well that bandwagoning is scummy.
xzhang wrote:
Vote: mymathkid that vote for kaz was the last straw. You have been bandwagoning hard the entire game. This being your second game is not an excuse. This is my third game. Make an actual contribution and I might take back this vote

As a scum, xzhang would not overtly bandwagon since he knows it is scummy. But what we see coming out of him is a more subtle kind of bandwagoning. He doesn't bandwagon with his votes mindlessly. Rather, he does what is at the core of bandwagoning, going with the flow. He does nothing to add new material or challenge town consensus. There is the veneer of contribution when in reality there is nothing. He repackages the reads of those around him and hides it better than blind bandwagoning.

While the mindless bandwagoning exhibited by mymathkid is indeed very bad play, such is common among newbies. Rather, the bandwagoning that is truly scummy is the kind that doesn't appear to be bandwagoning on first glance, but instead is able to slip by unnoticed. This is what xzhang has done.

Vote: xzhang
Mathkid bandwagoned but isn't scummy for it. Yaj bandwagoned but isn't scummy for it. Smart bandwagoned but isn't scummy for it. Xzhang also Bandwagoned, but actually is scummy for it. I do not understand that assertion. The phrases I underlined are all stated as facts. Facts can not be argued unlike opinions. However, I disagree with the underlined phrases causing the post to make little sense to me and there's no natural response because this shouldn't happen.

It is clear that bandwagoning by itself is not a useful indicator of scumminess, there are simply too many people who bandwagon. So, I attempt to differentiate between what I see as newby bandwagoning and bandwagoning that is scummy. I see bandwagoning like mymathkid's as too basic and consistent with newbie behavior. On the other hand, xzhang displays more sophisticationi because he recognizes bandwagoning is scummy and is actively hiding his lack of contribution.

On the other hand, xzhang displays more sophisticationi because he recognizes bandwagoning is scummy and is actively hiding his lack of contribution.
And that's what I disagree with. Newby bandwagoning and Scummy bandwagoning are neither mutually exclusive nor do they cover all bandwagoning.

post_rendered:
Thanks to cj for comodding and writing flavor and thanks to summit for bearing with me and answering all of my questions about modding this game!

Some comments about rules this game:
-2 days is a bit short for nights. I wanted to make night shorter as I tend to find night boring, especially as VT. But wolves didn't seem to have enough time to fully discuss and submit actions.
-10 days for day phases seems alright. Maybe a bit on the shorter side but I think it worked okay this game, and I didn't want the game to take forever.
-Activity rules felt fine. I had to prod people repeatedly, but the game was okay in activity, except for the last day phase, when I stopped caring after the first vote. Some people got kinda annoyed at my prods though.
-Daychat existed because it's nice for wolves to have. It was intended to help the wolves a little, since (I think?) mountainous with 2 wolves is 2v9. It wasn't used that much during the day, but I think it's still good to have.
-There were a couple minor OOT things that happened that I don't really remember, and I probably should have given out warnings for them but whatever.

About the actual gameplay (though I didn't actually read in detail): wolves played well. I would say something about scumhunting, but wolves were hard to find this game. Town came close to hanging Kazakata on day 4 (though I'm still not entirely sure why?). The night kills, particularly on avanders, pointed in their direction a little, I think.
post_canonical:
[color=#00f]Thanks to cj for comodding and writing flavor and thanks to summit for bearing with me and answering all of my questions about modding this game!

Some comments about rules this game:
-2 days is a bit short for nights. I wanted to make night shorter as I tend to find night boring, especially as VT. But wolves didn't seem to have enough time to fully discuss and submit actions.
-10 days for day phases seems alright. Maybe a bit on the shorter side but I think it worked okay this game, and I didn't want the game to take forever.
-Activity rules felt fine. I had to prod people repeatedly, but the game was okay in activity, except for the last day phase, when I stopped caring after the first vote. Some people got kinda annoyed at my prods though.
-Daychat existed because it's nice for wolves to have. It was intended to help the wolves a little, since (I think?) mountainous with 2 wolves is 2v9. It wasn't used that much during the day, but I think it's still good to have.
-There were a couple minor OOT things that happened that I don't really remember, and I probably should have given out warnings for them but whatever.

About the actual gameplay (though I didn't actually read in detail): wolves played well. I would say something about scumhunting, but wolves were hard to find this game. Town came close to hanging Kazakata on day 4 (though I'm still not entirely sure why?). The night kills, particularly on avanders, pointed in their direction a little, I think.[/color]
